meta.name: Spaghetti with Simple Tomato Sauce
meta.subtitle: Garlic, olive oil, crushed tomatoes, basil — weeknight classic

source.name: ssa-core
notes: Canonical baseline for Italian weeknight pasta in SSA catalogs.
Built to be adaptable by the RecipeAdapterService based on kitchen capabilities and doneness preferences.

yield.notes: About 1 heaping bowl per serving (includes sauce).

notes: Sauce can be doubled and frozen; cook pasta fresh for best texture.
Best results with quality canned tomatoes and finishing cheese.
If tomatoes are very acidic, add a small pinch of sugar or a knob of butter (optional).
Red pepper flakes optional for gentle heat.

optional.label: Box grater
optional.notes: If you don’t have a microplane.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: For pasta water: salt it until it tastes like the sea.
Reserve a pinch for sauce seasoning.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes (canned)
ingredients.notes: 28 oz can. Substitute passata for a smoother sauce.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste (optional)
ingredients.notes: 1 tablespoon. Adds depth; bloom briefly in oil.

ingredients.label: Fresh basil leaves
ingredients.notes: Tear by hand; add at the end.

ingredients.label: Parmesan (optional), grated
ingredients.notes: Serve at the table. Use Pecorino Romano for sharper bite.

steps.title: Mise en place
instructions: Fill a large pot with water and bring to a boil.
Thinly slice or mince the garlic. Tear basil leaves. Measure tomatoes and olive oil.
Set a large skillet on the stove (off heat for now).
checks: Garlic is prepped before the pasta goes in (prevents rushing).

steps.title: Salt the pasta water
instructions: Once the water boils, add kosher salt.
Keep at a steady boil while you start the sauce.
steps.notes: Salting the water is the main way pasta gets seasoned.

steps.title: Bloom garlic in olive oil
instructions: Warm olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat.
Add garlic (and red pepper flakes if using). Cook gently until fragrant, 45–90 seconds.
Do not brown the garlic; reduce heat if needed.
checks: Garlic is fragrant and pale golden at most (not browned).
Oil smells sweet and aromatic, not burnt.

steps.title: Build and simmer the tomato sauce
instructions: If using tomato paste, stir it into the garlic oil for 30–60 seconds to deepen flavor.
Add crushed tomatoes. Stir, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Simmer 8–12 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with black pepper and a pinch of salt if needed.
checks: Sauce thickens slightly and tastes less raw.
Balance: if too acidic, add a tiny pinch of sugar (optional).

steps.title: Cook the spaghetti
instructions: Add spaghetti to boiling salted water and stir for the first minute to prevent sticking.
Cook until al dente (start checking 2 minutes before the box time).
Reserve about 240 ml (1 cup) pasta water before draining.
checks: Pasta has a slight bite (al dente).

steps.title: Toss pasta with sauce and emulsify
instructions: Add drained pasta to the skillet with sauce.
Toss over medium heat for 1–2 minutes. Add splashes of reserved pasta water until the sauce clings and looks glossy.
Turn off heat. Stir in torn basil. Taste and adjust salt/pepper.
checks: Sauce coats noodles and looks slightly creamy from emulsification.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Top with grated Parmesan (optional). Add a drizzle of olive oil if desired.

notes: For a smoother sauce: use passata instead of crushed tomatoes.
For richer sauce: finish with 1–2 tbsp butter (optional; not dairy-free).
For extra aromatics: add 1/4 tsp dried oregano while simmering (optional).

adapt.notes: Bloom chili flakes with garlic; keep sauce simmer gentle to avoid bitterness.
Use nutritional yeast for cheesy savoriness; finish with extra olive oil.

notes: Reheat with a splash of water to loosen sauce.
Freeze sauce alone up to 3 months for best quality.
Pasta texture degrades when frozen; cook fresh pasta when possible.
Refresh with olive oil + splash of water while reheating.
